title: echinocereus multangularis var. pallidior
intro: Echinocereus multangularis var. pallidior is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Echinocereus multangularis species, commonly referred to as the "butterfly cactus." This cactus is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and is known for its unique, pale yellow stems and vibrant pink flowers. The medicinal properties of Echinocereus multangularis var. pallidior include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been traditionally used to make herbal preparations, including teas and salves, which are said to promote overall health and well-being, and provide relief from various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin irritations.
